column5: Google Core Updates 2026: March, June, September major changes
column6: March 2026 Core Update = most substantial ranking changes in recent years. June 2026 Helpful Content Update refined detection of content created for search engines vs users. September 2026 Page Experience Update introduced stricter Core Web Vitals + mobile usability requirements, particularly affecting e-commerce and news sites.
column7: 1. Audit content for user intent over keyword stuffing 2. Ensure Core Web Vitals compliance (LCP CLS FID) 3. Clean site architecture + structured data 4. Mobile usability optimization for complex layouts 5. Focus on topical depth + practical answers over thin content
column14: Core Web Vitals mandatory. Helpful content = survive, thin content = penalized. E-commerce/news sites most affected.

column5: 43% citation rate increase with dedicated GEO tools, 25% search volume drop
column6: Brands using dedicated Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) tools see 43% increase in AI citation rates. Traditional search volume predicted to drop 25% by 2026. 73% of B2B buyers use ChatGPT/Perplexity in research. McKinsey predicts $750B consumer spending through AI search by 2028.
column7: 1. Add specific statistics with methodology notes + sources (22-28% visibility boost) 2. Restructure high-performing content with 120-180 word sections between hierarchical headers (40% citation improvement) 3. Update high-value pages with visible last updated timestamps + fresh data (30% more Perplexity citations) 4. Ungate key statistics from PDF lead magnets (AI can't read locked content) 5. Use GEO tracking tools (OmniSEO Otterly.ai Rankscale Ahrefs Brand Radar)
column14: HIGHEST ROI GEO tactics. 43% citation increase proof. McKinsey $750B consumer spending forecast validates shift.

column5: Only 11% of domains cited by both ChatGPT and Perplexity - platform-specific optimization required
column6: Analysis of 680M citations: Only 11% of domains cited by both ChatGPT and Perplexity. ChatGPT heavily uses Wikipedia (1.3M mentions), G2 (196K), Forbes (181K), Amazon (133K). Perplexity: Reddit = 46.7% of citations, averages 6.61 citations per response, favors YouTube. Google AI Overview: Reddit = 21% of citations.
column7: 1. Platform-specific optimization: Wikipedia backlinks for ChatGPT visibility 2. Reddit participation for Perplexity citations (46.7% of citations) 3. YouTube content for Perplexity preference 4. G2 reviews for ChatGPT citations (B2B SaaS) 5. Different content strategies per platform (not one-size-fits-all)
column14: HIGHEST value insight. 680M citation analysis proves platform-specific strategies required. Reddit = Perplexity gold.

column5: Citing sources + statistics + quotations = 30-40% AI visibility improvement
column6: Princeton research: Top GEO optimization methods (citing sources, adding statistics, including quotations) improve AI visibility 30-40% vs unoptimized content. Inverted Pyramid approach critical. Convert paragraph comparisons to HTML tables (LLMs prefer structured data). E-E-A-T (Experience Expertise Authoritativeness Trustworthiness) remains strategic cornerstone.
column7: 1. Lead with Zero-Token answers (inverted pyramid) 2. Add inline citations for all claims 3. Include specific statistics with sources 4. Use HTML tables for comparisons 5. Add quotations from experts 6. Strengthen E-E-A-T signals on all content
column14: 30-40% visibility boost validated by Princeton research. Low effort, high impact. Tables + citations + stats = AI preference.

column5: ASO 2026: AI-powered search, semantic matching, 70 custom product pages
column6: Apple Natural Language Processing interprets conversational queries (apps to help me relax before bed). Semantic matching > keyword stuffing. Apple doubled custom product pages from 35 to 70, can manually assign keywords to each CPP for search eligibility. Battery optimization = ranking factor (March 1 2026 deadline). First 2 screenshots most critical elements.
column7: 1. Write app metadata naturally + conversational (AI NLP optimization) 2. Use all 70 custom product pages with keyword assignments 3. Ensure battery optimization compliance (ranking factor) 4. A/B test first 2 screenshots (highest impact) 5. Implement App Intents framework for Siri/Spotlight/widget discovery 6. Continuous experimentation over one-time optimization
column14: 70 custom product pages = 2x keyword targeting. Battery optimization now ranking factor. Screenshots > keyword tweaks for conversion.

column5: 90% of featured apps have 4.0+ rating, visual-first decision making
column6: Ratings/reviews remain critical ASO trend 2026: 90% of featured apps have 4.0+ rating. Visual-first decision making: icons, first screenshots, preview videos carry disproportionate weight. Creative testing produces faster gains than incremental keyword changes. Continuous experimentation cycle: hypothesis, test, insight, scale.
column7: 1. Maintain 4.0+ rating minimum (featured app requirement) 2. Prioritize creative testing over keyword optimization 3. Invest in high-quality first 2 screenshots 4. Run A/B tests on icon variations 5. Implement continuous experimentation workflow (weekly tests) 6. Use preview videos for conversion boost
column14: 4.0+ rating = table stakes for featuring. Creative testing > keyword tweaks for faster gains. Continuous experimentation required.

column5: GEO multimodal optimization: search via photo/video as likely as text in 2026
column6: As of 2026, search moved beyond text - fully conversational visual modes in Google Gemini and ChatGPT. Input for search just as likely to be photo or video clip as typed query. GEO emphasizes brand visibility + mentions (not rankings). Treat AI as branding channel. Manage GEO separately from SEO.
column7: 1. Optimize images for AI understanding (alt text
column9: context) 2. Create video content optimized for AI extraction 3. Focus on brand visibility mentions over rankings 4. Separate GEO strategy from SEO strategy 5. Track brand mentions in AI responses 6. Use multimodal content (text + images + video combined)
column16: Multimodal shift validated. Photos/videos = search inputs now. Brand visibility > rankings in AI era. Separate GEO team needed.

column5: AI Overviews reduce traditional organic traffic, GEO = branding channel
column6: AI Overviews already affecting impressions + clicks. By 2026, summaries more prominent, reducing traditional organic traffic. Brands that establish trusted sources included more often in generated answers. GEO changing where discovery happens (search engines → social + AI). Plan now to own visibility in 2026.
column7: 1. Accept traffic shift from organic search to AI citations 2. Build brand authority for trusted source positioning 3. Optimize for inclusion in AI summaries over click-through 4. Diversify discovery channels (social + AI + search) 5. Measure brand mentions in AI responses as KPI 6. Focus on thought leadership content for authority
column14: Traffic shift inevitable. AI citations > organic clicks. Brand authority = inclusion in AI answers. Measure mentions, not rankings.

column5: Structured content + clarity + authority = LLM preference, succinct answers win
column6: Generative engines deliver summarized + synthesized info for quick answers (not lengthy lead-ups). GEO emphasizes clarity, authority, structure. Fact-rich, easily digestible content wins. LLMs prefer structured data for best X vs Y queries. Ungate key statistics to feed AI context window.
column8: rewrite intros for Zero-Token answers 2. Convert comparisons to HTML tables 3. Ungate statistics from PDF lead magnets 4. Provide direct answers in first 100 words 5. Use bullet lists + tables for scannability 6. Add FAQ sections with natural question phrasing
column15: Succinct wins. Zero-Token answers = AI preference. Ungate data for citations. Structured content (tables bullets FAQs) > paragraphs.

column5: 9 GEO tips for 2026: schema markup, Q&A format, author authority
column6: Top 9 GEO tactics: 1) Schema markup for all content types 2) Q&A format content 3) Author bylines with credentials 4) Cite authoritative sources 5) Use simple language 6) Include data/statistics 7) Update content regularly 8) Internal linking 9) Monitor AI search performance. Half of consumers already use ChatGPT/Perplexity for discovery.
column7: 1. Implement schema markup on all pages (Article FAQ HowTo Product) 2. Restructure content as Q&A format 3. Add author pages with credentials (E-E-A-T) 4. Cite sources inline with links 5. Simplify language (8th grade reading level) 6. Add statistics to every article 7. Update timestamps + content monthly 8. Strong internal linking structure 9. Track AI citations with tools
column14: Comprehensive GEO checklist. 50% of consumers use AI for discovery = massive shift. Schema + Q&A + author authority = core.
